title: salix stylosa var. ovata
intro: Salix stylosa var. ovata, also known as River Willow or White Willow, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the eastern parts of Australia. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which are attributed to the presence of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Salix stylosa var. ovata include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as aiding in the treatment of fever and rheumatism. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used to relieve various ailments, including sore muscles and joints.
